meta.setting: Print shops, muddy street corners, boardinghouses, courthouse steps, and French Gulch alleys
meta.rdr_clone_target: RDR-clone western with rumor, public accusation, reputation, law heat, and hard choices

research_grounding.title: California State Parks Shasta State Historic Park
research_grounding.use: Northern California mining commerce, courthouse town life, and public street texture.

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Mining communities, roads, settlement pressure, and public rumor context.

generation_prompt.task: Generate the Press Boys cast page for the 1899 Jefferson State western packet. This JSON contains shape and guidance only.
page_shape: square image reference immediately after the title
opening blockquote with invented print-shop note, wet-ink warning, or street-corner saying generated later
H2 archetype subtitle in the register of Ink Runners
one or two compact lore paragraphs introducing newspaper runners, apprentice printers, gossip carriers, and sellers of public truth by the sheet
H3 Role with a short table-facing function phrase
H3 Traits with two to four terse signs or habits
10 H4 headings, each followed later by a brief playable move, leverage, cost, edge, or complication
H3 Camp Say closing blockquote with plain meaning generated later
generation_guidance: Generate press boys as fast, nosy, underpaid young workers around print shops, courthouse steps, and street corners.
Tie future moves to early editions, overheard names, smudged proof, public accusation, runaway gossip, hidden kindness, printer debt, and knowing who bought silence.
Keep them energetic without making them cute mascots.
Make printed news physical and dangerous: wet ink, folded sheets, public shame, retractions, and rumors that outrun horses.
Do not include final move names or final move text in this JSON.
generation_prompt.hand_cut_note: Their hands should always be dirty with ink and somebody else's business. In a small town, news is not printed; it is weaponized wet.
generation_prompt.table_spark: A press-boy scene should ask what got printed early, who paid for a correction, and which rider learns the headline too late.
must_preserve: Use 1899 western print-shop and street language only.
Keep news practical, social, and dangerous.
The generated page must include image path cast/images/press-boys.png.
No finished page prose, final moves, or final sayings belong in this prompt file.
avoid: do not mention any prior packet, inherited taxonomy, or non-western source language
do not include medieval, enchanted, sacred, or fake-tribal framing
do not make young workers cute mascots or comic relief only
do not use modern media, cameras, radio, modern slang, vehicles, or clean theme-park staging
self_check: The future markdown follows the exact heading sequence.
The future page is about print, rumor, public accusation, reputation, and table action.
The prompt contains no finished page prose.
All language belongs to grounded 1899 Jefferson State western play.
